They say this is hard or that's hard or— and I— I really don't think that most things are hard. And— and I'm going— except for this dick. You know what I'm talking about? No, but I think most things are super simple, but we— we— we make them hard or we believe they're hard and— and— and because we believe they are, they are, right?
[01:58]

And people will come to me, you know, bitching and moaning. I can't eat right. I can't. I'm like, the truth is they just— they just don't want it bad enough. And that's okay, right? You know, it's like— I mean, aside from some people with some genetic predispositions, but that's— that's pretty rare, right? It's just really controlling the amount of food you put in your mouth.
[02:32]

Everybody in this room has done stuff way harder than that. You know what I'm saying? It's like right above not sh***ing on yourself, pissing your pants, right? But that's difficult for a lot of people to comprehend because they believe it's difficult and they really believe it.

[06:44] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "STEP 1: What amount of effort would make failure impossible?"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Framework visual de 3 passos, ênfase no primeiro passo sobre esforço que torna falha impossível
[06:48]
So, step one in this process of making everything easy is don't do what you think would accomplish the goal. Do— ask yourself what amount of effort would make failure impossible, right? It's not what do I— It's not what do I have to do to accomplish the goal, but what will I have to do? What kind of effort would I have to put in that would make failure impossible?
[07:14]
You know, it wasn't long ago. Somebody reminded me this story earlier. Yeah, it— I was— I was in LA. [07:56]

[09:02]
All right. So, that's step one. So, so what does that look like in reality? Step two, you figure out— So, after you figure out, okay, what amount of effort would I have to put in to make failure impossible? And like list it out, right? [09:38]
I track exactly how many calories I burned with the Aura ring. I see a lot of you guys have these devices and sh** on wombs and sh**. If you track exactly how many calories you eat and exactly how many you burn every day and you make sure you're in a deficit when you want to lose weight, like you can't fail, right?
[10:24]
So if I— I— I wasn't worried about that. So that goes to my second point, right? Once you find— Okay, no, let me explain it. So some of you guys have like— you guys have goals, right? This is going to be like a trophy. This represent goals. I know you guys are thinking, damn, man, Brandon is such a good artist. Maybe you should have pursued that. But so this is a trophy, right? This is— this represents your goal.
[10:50]
So a lot of people I see them, they look at their f*ing goals and they see how far they are away from them or they see other people who's accomplish them and they get sad and start moping around like a little um— b**. Yeah. And then they— they— they get depressed and you know that they don't— and maybe they give up, right? We've all kind of probably been there at some point in our lives, right?
[11:16]
But that's because they're looking at the goal in relation to where they are, right? But the problem with that is the goal is what's called— it's just a result, right? Me being in the shape I am is just a result of the other sh** I was doing. You know what I'm saying? So this— it's a— it's what's called a— a lagging indicator, right? There's leading indicators and there's lagging indicators.
[11:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Leading Indicators vs Lagging Indicators"
NÚMEROS: 50% close rate (calls booked)
VISUAL: Comparação visual entre indicadores antecedentes (leading) e resultados (lagging), exemplo de sales team metrics, show rates, close rates
[11:44]
So if you guys have sales teams, right? and you— your sales teams are— you got all the numbers and sh**. If you see— sh** are, you know, got high show rates and low closing rates and you know all these things, especially what my man was talking about like he says he can— he can tell when somebody's about to fall off, right? They're not said— they're not showing up and not engaged. That's a leading indicator. So he can— he can tell what's going to happen, right?
[12:12]
The lagging indicator is just a result. It's really just a reflection of what you did in the past. Does that make sense? We're all living the results of what we did in the past, right? So if we want our future to be different than our past, we got to do different sh**, right? So instead of focusing on the— the— the results, like don't focus on them at all. That's what I do. I don't even think about the sh**, right? I focus on the leading indicators, right?
[12:42]
So how do I put this? You write down all the— remember in step one we said what would we have to do to make failure impossible, right? You start just listening— list— listening to sh** out the l— the— the— leading indicators and focus exclusively on those things, right? And that's going to do two things for you. One, if you're focusing on just accomplishing the— the— the leading indicators each day, whether it's for a fitness goal, it would be like, okay, I got to burn this many calories and I got to eat this many calories and hit this many amount of protein. Just as an example, if you focus on that and you do that today, then today's a victory, right?
[13:24]
And if you do that every day for a week, that— that week's a victory. If you do that, you know, four weeks in a row, that month is a victorious month. You do that for sh** 12 months in a row, your whole year, you— you'll be in the best shape you ever been in your life. It's just math. It's not even difficult. Is this making sense?
[13:44]
It's really just f***ing math, right?
[13:48]
But the beauty of that is when you accomplish these things every day, you will feel good today. Right? This sh**— even if he hits this goal, he's like not happy until he gets the goal. He's made a contract with himself that he's going to be unhappy until he hits the goal. But it doesn't have to be like that. It's just like, "Oh sh**— I'm just going to focus on winning today." And then you can do that every day. Does that make sense?
[14:14]
Right. Just focus on the leading indicators exclusively. Like exclusively— like this, this is going to sound weird, man, but like— like I hit— like last night over— overnight. I hit a million followers on YouTube.
[14:28]
Oh, thank you. Thank you. I appreciate that. Yeah.

[23:14] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Titanic Compartment Principle"
NÚMEROS: $20,000 (funeral)
VISUAL: Metáfora do Titanic - compartimentos no navio para evitar afundamento total, aplicado à vida (não deixar uma área ruim afetar todas as outras)
[23:16]
Like my cousin died last year. We grew up together. My best friend— went to high school together. We got sent away to military school in high school cuz we got in a bunch of trouble, you know, just like normal sh**. You know, skipping school, fights, shootouts, you know, normal southside Chicago sh**. And um— they sent us to military school and we went to the same college together. Then we both lived in New York together. We both moved down here. He was like like a— my brother, right? And he died in a car accident. And that sh** really f***ed me up.
[23:52]
And this may sound cold. I still hit my sh** lead to indicators every day. Even though I was crying, mourning, right? Because I knew that because I do— I do these things every day now. I got enough money I can just pay for his funeral, right? Like not everybody can, you know, find out, hey, you need $20,000 tomorrow for a funeral. Like, not everybody can just say, yeah, all right, go ahead. Right? I know that the only reason I can do that is because I hit those leading indicators. I don't f*** around. You know what I'm saying?
[24:28]
And you can be sad, you can be depressed, you can be tired and still hit those numbers. You know what I'm saying? Because you can't let— you know back in the day, y'all heard of the boat. There's this boat called the Titanic. Anybody heard of that? All right, cool. This is going to make sense to you then. All right. Before the— the Titanic, they used to build boats, right? And they, you know, they would crash and they would flood, hit— hit some sh**. But then after the— the— the Titanic crash, he said, you know what? Let's start— let's start making compartments in these sh**. So like if— if this part cra— gets a hole in it, it doesn't sink the whole ship.
[25:08]
That's how you got to think about your sh** life, right? Don't let one bad thing f*** up everything. Some people, oh, my girlfriend broke up with me. That's how I got fat and lost my business. I'm like, what? What? Yo, man. What— that b**** got to do with how much you eat in your business, man? Stop being a b****. right? You got to focus on that sh**. Like, you see, you got to— don't let one part of your life f*** up every part of your life. 📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
1 milhão de seguidores no YouTube
20 anos como personal trainer
20 anos tracking calorias e macros
3 YouTube shorts por dia
5 vídeos por semana no YouTube
1 live por semana no YouTube
1 ano de consistência nessa frequência
3 meses consecutivos de recordes de faturamento
50% close rate em calls agendadas
168 horas por semana disponíveis
84 horas de trabalho (12h/dia x 7 dias)
56 horas de sono (8h/noite)
28 horas restantes para outras atividades
$20,000 para funeral do primo
$3 milhões/ano do pai (equivalente a $8 milhões ajustado pela inflação em 2000)
15 anos de trabalho do pai para atingir sucesso
300mg de cafeína diária
16 anos usando cafeína
40 anos de idade (Brandon)
Multi-milhões em patrimônio
25 anos lendo "Law of Attraction in Money"
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Framework de 3 Passos para Tornar Tudo Fácil:
Esforço que torna falha impossível - Não fazer o que você acha que vai funcionar, mas sim o que tornaria impossível falhar (metáfora: não dar um tapa no inseto, mas fumegar o prédio inteiro)
Leading Indicators - Focar exclusivamente nos indicadores antecedentes, não nos resultados (lagging indicators). Listar todas as ações diárias necessárias e executá-las
Tracking System - Criar planilha/sistema de tracking diário, colocar tudo no calendário Google, automatizar o máximo possível
Sistema de Tracking Pessoal:
Calorias consumidas (tracking em tempo real via app)
Calorias queimadas (automático via Oura Ring)
Proteína ingerida
Horas de sono (automático via Oura Ring)
Humor ao longo do dia (IMood Journal app)
Tempo com namorada
Tempo com filho
Horas de estudo
Tempo gasto em conteúdo
Fórmulas para calcular consistência (80%, 90%, 100%)
Sistema de Tracking da Equipe:
Planilhas diárias obrigatórias
Tracking de humor da equipe
Horas de sono dos vendedores
Situação pessoal/relacionamentos
Leading indicators aparecem quando evitam preencher ou atrasam
Princípio do Titanic (Compartimentalização):
Não deixar uma área da vida afetar todas as outras
Manter indicadores principais mesmo em tragédias
Continuar executando durante luto, depressão, cansaço
Filosofia de Foco:
"Vencer o dia" ao invés de focar na meta final
Mês vitorioso = mais vitórias do que derrotas
Não precisa de dias perfeitos, apenas consistência
Remover necessidade de validação externa
Lista de Ódio (Motivação):
Não é raiva, é "vamos ver o que acontece"
Usar como combustível sem emoção negativa
Pensar "como eles ficariam felizes se eu falhasse?"
Provar eles errados através de resultados
ESTRUTURA:
Calendário Estruturado:
Toda vida planejada no Google Calendar
Leading indicators agendados diariamente
Horário de acordar, dormir, comer, treinar
Alarmes para preparar para dormir
Pouco espaço para distrações
Vida estruturada = menos problemas
Hierarquia de Prioridades:
Fazer dinheiro (sempre #1)
Filho
Fitness
Relacionamentos
Tudo mais vem depois
Gestão de Tempo:
12h trabalho/dia = 84h/semana
8h sono/noite = 56h/semana
1h treino/dia = 7h/semana
Sobram 21h para tudo mais
Sem tempo para TV, filmes, videogames, recreação excessiva
Princípio da Expansão vs Intensidade:
Sucesso não vem de home runs
Vem de pequeno esforço por longo período
Metáfora do Grand Canyon (rio Colorado)
Não precisa mudar nada, só manter consistência
Expansão da equipe, não intensidade individual
Disciplina Proporcional ao Sucesso:
Metáfora do carro: velocidade baixa = pouca atenção, velocidade alta = máximo foco
Mais sucesso = mais oportunidades de f*** up
Mais leverage = mais formas criativas de arruinar vida
Necessidade de ser mais "on point" conforme cresce
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Recordes mensais consecutivos (3 meses seguidos)
Expectativa de ano recorde novamente
50% close rate em calls agendadas (não excluindo no-shows ou cancelamentos)
Investimento em escola privada cara para filho em Georgia
Capacidade de pagar $20k de funeral sem planejamento
Pai perdeu $3M/ano ($8M ajustado) por má gestão e indulgência
Brandon "apenas" multi-milhonário aos 40 (poderia ter mais se não tivesse se distraído)
SUPLEMENTOS/STACK:
300mg cafeína ao começar o dia
Modafinil (bloqueia receptores de cansaço, menos viciante que Adderall)
Adderall ocasionalmente quando necessário
Testosterona (injetável se níveis baixos)
Nicotina (patches, vaping) - cognitive enhancer
Não afeta sono (aclimatado após anos de uso)
LIVROS RECOMENDADOS:
"Influence" - Robert Cialdini (leitura mensal)
"Pitch Anything" (lido centenas de vezes)
"Secrets of Power Negotiating" - Roger Dawson (livro secreto)
"Law of Attraction in Money" - Esther Hicks (lido por 25 anos, mais lido de todos)
"Awaken the Giant Within" - Tony Robbins (lido aos 17, mudou vida)
EQUIPAMENTOS:
Oura Ring (tracking automático de sono e calorias queimadas)
Balança na bolsa (pesar comida)
Apps de tracking de calorias
IMood Journal app
Google Calendar
Notion/Planilhas para tracking
INSIGHTS CHAVE:
"Amadores praticam até acertar, profissionais praticam até não conseguirem errar"
"Se você argumenta pelas suas limitações, você as mantém"
"Qualquer coisa que você tracka, você melhora"
"Recomeçar é mais difícil do que continuar durante tragédia"
"Querer o negócio mas não precisar dele"
"Terminator de meia-idade negro" (auto-imagem)
"Melhor que todos" (motivação interna vs recompensas externas)
"F*** as estrelas, a lua, Júpiter - deixe os perdedores se preocuparem com Vênus"
DURAÇÃO: ~1h 07min FORMATO: Palestra em mastermind, apresentação interativa com Q&A extenso
